steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The study found that plots with limited logging are more robust than unlogged plots. The blank must state what these results suggest — a conclusion that follows directly from that finding without adding unsupported claims.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'logging may be useful for maintaining healthy forests, provided it is limited.': directly matches the study’s finding that limited logging produces more robust plots. ✓
  • Choice B — 'other forest management strategies are more effective than limited logging.': the passage compares limited logging to no logging, not to other strategies. ✗
  • Choice C — 'as time passes, it will be difficult to know whether limited logging has any benefits.': contradicts the passage, which presents positive results, not uncertainty about future findings. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the best way to support forest health may be to leave large forests entirely untouched.': directly contradicts the study, which shows untouched plots are less robust than limited-logging plots. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
